steps.title: Identify the hypothesis's prediction
steps.content: The second team predicts that intermediate-headed fish will be more likely than narrow-headed fish to detect obstructions creating large vortices in low-visibility. More likely to detect = fewer collisions in a test.

steps.title: Find the study showing the predicted advantage
steps.content:
Choice C directly compares both fish types in the specified conditions and shows narrow-headed fish bumping into more obstructions.

The others either show equal performance (A), test only narrow-headed fish (B), or measure within-species variation (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
